title: Roasted Spaghetti Squash
description: Oven-roasted spaghetti squash shredded into tender noodle-like strands. Serve with your favorite pasta sauce, with or without meat.

ingredients.name: spaghetti squash (heavy for its size, firm yellow rind)

instructions.text: Preheat oven to 400°F. Line a baking sheet with parchment or foil.
Cut squash in half lengthwise -- use a sharp knife and take care, the rind is hard. Scoop out seeds and stringy pulp with a spoon.
Drizzle cut sides with olive oil and season with salt and pepper. Place cut-side down on the baking sheet. Optionally add 1/4 cup water to the pan to create steam, which helps soften the strands.
Roast 30–45 minutes depending on size. Check doneness by piercing the skin with a fork -- it should go through easily and the flesh should shred readily into strands.

Let cool a few minutes. Use a fork to scrape the flesh into noodle-like strands. For extra softness, sauté strands briefly in a skillet with a little butter or olive oil, or microwave for 1–2 minutes.
Serve topped with your favorite pasta sauce, with or without meat.

notes:
Size matters for timing: a small squash (2–3 lbs) is done in 30 minutes; a large one (4–5 lbs) may need 45 minutes or more.

The water on the pan is optional but recommended if you want softer strands rather than slightly al dente ones.

Leftover strands keep refrigerated for 3–4 days and reheat well in a skillet.
